source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.

items.final_wording: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

added_items.final_wording: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

added_items.rationale: Supports sleep-pattern and daytime-function review for sleep hygiene discussion; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.
